instructions.description: Welcome and introduction: Explains the experiment structure, duration, compensation, and the lottery investment task rules including payoff structure, endowment, and how the die determines outcomes.
instructions.html_content:
Introduction  Welcome to our study of decision-making. The study will last about 7 minutes. All the money you earn is yours to keep. You will be compensated $0.30 for the successful completion of this study.

In this study, you will play an investment game over several rounds. At the start of the study, you will be given an $8.00 endowment. In each round, you will have $2.00 available to invest in a lottery. You may invest any amount from $0 to $2.00 (in $0.25 increments).

The lottery works as follows: Before each round, you will be assigned a "success number" between 1 and 6. A die will then be rolled. If the die matches your success number, you WIN 7 times your investment. If the die does not match your success number, you LOSE your investment. Any amount you do not invest is kept.

You will play multiple rounds. Your total earnings will include your $8.00 endowment plus any investment gains or minus any investment losses.

screens.description: Round 1: Participant chooses how much of $2.00 to invest in the lottery (slider, $0–$2 in $0.25 increments).
Round 2: Participant chooses how much of $2.00 to invest in the lottery (slider, $0–$2 in $0.25 increments).
Round 3: Participant chooses how much of $2.00 to invest in the lottery (slider, $0–$2 in $0.25 increments). After this round, the treatment condition (realized_loss or paper_loss) is applied.
Round 4 (final): Participant chooses how much of $2.00 to invest in the lottery after treatment condition has been applied. Key outcome measure: investment level and change from round 3.
